This file is the deliberate negative-fixture for the gitleaks scanner.

If you remove it, the secrets-scan gate has nothing to prove against. Every
finding in here is a FAKE — audited and pinned via .gitleaksignore (and the
leaked-secret.txt path is allowlisted in .gitleaks.toml).

The point is: when a developer commits a REAL leak, gitleaks fires. When the
canary stays at the values below, gitleaks stays silent. That's the wiring
proof.

AWS Access Key fake (note: deliberately invalid — RFC reserved range):
  AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE

GitHub Personal Access Token (fake — would be rejected by gh.com):
  ghp_0000000000000000000000000000000000000000

Generic password assignment (fake):
  PASSWORD="not-a-real-password-just-a-canary-value"

Don't edit this file without re-running `make secrets-scan` to confirm the
scanner's behavior hasn't drifted.
